*   Comment
*   Published: 19 August 2015

# Policy: Hurricane Katrina’s lessons for the world

*   [Edward B. Barbier](#auth-Edward_B_-Barbier-Aff1)[1](#Aff1) 

[*Nature*](/) **volume 524**, pages 285–287 (2015) [Cite this article](#citeas) 

[Save article](/articles/524285a/save-research?_csrf=MdvwIpUCEg-6-xkO-HO2LzKIGexNz6Rn)

[View saved research](/saved-research) 

*   2184 Accesses

*   22 Citations

*   255 Altmetric

*   [Metrics details](/articles/524285a/metrics)

### Subjects

*   [Conservation biology](/subjects/conservation)
*   [Policy](/subjects/policy)

Ten years after the devastation of the US Gulf coast, Edward B. Barbier calls for coastal protection plans like that adopted by Louisiana for the world's most vulnerable nations.

[Access through your institution](https://wayf.springernature.com?redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.nature.com%2Farticles%2F524285a)

## References

1.  Knabb, R. D., Rhome J, R. &amp; Brown, D. P. *Tropical Cyclone Report: Hurricane Katrina* (US National Hurricane Center, 2005).

2.  Coastal Protection and Restoration Authority of Louisiana. *Louisiana's Comprehensive Master Plan for a Sustainable Coast* (Office of Coastal Protection and Restoration, 2012).

3.  Gulf Coast Ecosystem Recovery Task Force. *Gulf of Mexico Regional Ecosystem Restoration Strategy* (Gulf Coast Ecosystem Recovery Task Force, 2011).

4.  Woodruff, J. D., Irish, J. L. &amp; Carnargo, S. J. *Nature* **504**, 44–52 (2012).

    [Article](https://doi.org/10.1038%2Fnature12855)  [ADS](http://adsabs.harvard.edu/cgi-bin/nph-data_query?link_type=ABSTRACT&bibcode=2013Natur.504...44W)  [Google Scholar](http://scholar.google.com/scholar_lookup?&title=&journal=Nature&doi=10.1038%2Fnature12855&volume=504&pages=44-52&publication_year=2012&author=Woodruff%2CJD&author=Irish%2CJL&author=Carnargo%2CSJ) 

5.  Center for International Earth Science Information Network. *Low Elevation Coastal Zone (LECZ) Urban-Rural Population and Land Area Estimates, Version 2* (NASA Socioeconomic Data and Applications Center, 2013); available at [http://dx.doi.org/10.7927/h4mw2f2j](https://doi.org/10.7927/h4mw2f2j)

6.  Dasgupta, S. et al. *Vulnerability of Bangladesh to Cyclones in Changing Climate: Potential Damages and Adaptation Cost* (World Bank, 2010).

7.  DasGupta, R. & Shaw, R. *Ocean Coast. Mgmt* **80**, 107–118 (2013).

    [Article](https://doi.org/10.1016%2Fj.ocecoaman.2013.04.010)  [Google Scholar](http://scholar.google.com/scholar_lookup?&title=&journal=Ocean%20Coast.%20Mgmt&doi=10.1016%2Fj.ocecoaman.2013.04.010&volume=80&pages=107-118&publication_year=2013&author=DasGupta%2CR&author=Shaw%2CR) 

8.  Rawlani, A. K. &amp; Sovacool, B. K. *Mitig. Adapt. Strat. Glob. Change* **16**, 845–863 (2011).

    [Article](https://link.springer.com/doi/10.1007/s11027-011-9298-6)  [Google Scholar](http://scholar.google.com/scholar_lookup?&title=&journal=Mitig.%20Adapt.%20Strat.%20Glob.%20Change&doi=10.1007%2Fs11027-011-9298-6&volume=16&pages=845-863&publication_year=2011&author=Rawlani%2CAK&author=Sovacool%2CBK) 

9.  Cinner, J. E. et al. *Glob. Environ. Change* **22**, 12–20 (2012).

    [Article](https://doi.org/10.1016%2Fj.gloenvcha.2011.09.018)  [ADS](http://adsabs.harvard.edu/cgi-bin/nph-data_query?link_type=ABSTRACT&bibcode=2016GCBio..22...12C)  [Google Scholar](http://scholar.google.com/scholar_lookup?&title=&journal=Glob.%20Environ.%20Change&doi=10.1016%2Fj.gloenvcha.2011.09.018&volume=22&pages=12-20&publication_year=2012&author=Cinner%2CJE)

## Author information

### Authors and Affiliations

1.  Edward B. Barbier is professor of economics at the University of Wyoming in Laramie, Wyoming, USA. His book Nature and Wealth: Overcoming Environmental Scarcity and Inequality will be published this year.,

    Edward B. Barbier
